title: Frozen Tissue Dissociation for Single-Nucleus RNA-Seq
author: Linas Mazutis

steps.title: Prepare the laminar hood and the grinding glassware
steps.instruction: Prepare the laminar hood and the grinding glassware: Clean the surface and glassware thoroughly with DRNAse Free Reagent Spray followed by 70% ethanol. Bring the ice bucket in the cell culture hood and spray it with DRNAse Free Reagent. Place fully assembled Dounce homogenizer on ice and to let it cool. Bring sterile blade in the hood.

steps.title: Prepare a frozen tissue
steps.instruction: Prepare a frozen tissue: On a dry ice, place a frozen tissue on a Petri dish and cut it into two or three smaller pieces (e.g. the size of which corresponds to a half-rice grain).

steps.title: Prepare Lysis buffer
steps.instruction: Prepare Lysis buffer: Transfer 600 µL, Lysis Buffer in 1.5 mL tube, add 1.0 µL of DEPC and vortex vigorously until solution becomes cloudy and homogenous. DEPC is not soluble in water and it will form layer of droplets on top. Therefore, mix vigorously to disperse DEPC droplets. Transfer ready-to-use lysis solution in 2 mL grinding glass tube.

steps.title: Grind the tissue
steps.instruction: Grind the tissue: Place the small piece(s) of frozen tissue in 2 mL grinding glass tube. Using a large clearance pestle (Tube A) for the initial sample grinding gently move pestle up and down 10-15 times. Do not remove pestle out of the liquid while grinding the tissue, avoid creating bubbles, which may form if pestle is being moved too quickly. Next, use a small clearance pestle (Tube B) and grind tissue further by movinig up and down for 10-15 times. Tissue should be completely minced and homogenized. Certain tissue types might be elastic and hard to break down completely. In such case move to next step. Keep the sample in a glass tube on ice.

steps.title: Record experimental details
steps.instruction: Record experimental details: Write down if something unusual happened, tissue did not dissociate, etc.

steps.title: IMPORTANT
steps.instruction: IMPORTANT : keep nuclei suspension cool at all times. NOTE: For centrifugation use a swinging bucket centrifugure and 1.5 ml (or 2.0 ml) Protein LoBind Eppendorf tubes placed inside the 50 mL Falcon tube.

steps.title: Centrifugation
steps.instruction: Centrifugation: Spin down the tube in a swinging bucket centrifuge at 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes . Remove the supernatant without disrupting the pellet, the size of which should be smaller than in the previous step.

steps.title: Centrifugation
steps.instruction: Centrifugation: Spin down the dissociated tissue suspension in a swinging bucket centrifuge at 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes . There should be a clearly visible pellet. If you don't see one, check the settings on your centrifuge. Remove and save supernatant from the centrifuged nuclei pellet. Do not disturb the pellet, leave ~ 10-20 uL of supernatant on top.

steps.title: Straining
steps.instruction: Straining: Place 5 mL Falcon Round-bottom tube (typically used for FACS) with blue Snap Cap strainer on ice. Strain the nuclei suspension through 35 µm Cell Strainer Snap Cap and collect flow through fraction into the tube. If it is difficult to aspirate the nuclei suspension by pipette another option is to pour all suspension out of 2 mL homogenizer tube directly onto the blue cap. However, care must be taken to not introduce any ice that might be stuck on the outside walls of a homogenizer tube. Transfer the filtered solution to 1.5 mL Protein LoBind Eppendorf tube, keep on ice.

steps.title: Resuspension
steps.instruction: Resuspension: Re-suspend the pellet in 1 mL, Nuclei Wash Buffer using 1000 µL pipette.

steps.title: Resuspension and straining
steps.instruction: Resuspension and straining: Re-suspend the nuclei pellet in 1 mL, Nuclei Wash Buffer , mix well using 1000 µL pipette until suspension becomes homogenious. The volume of buffer at this step can be adjusted as needed (e.g. 0.5 ml). Filter the nuclei suspension through 5 mL FACS Snap Cap strainer and collect into the tube.

steps.title: Nuclei evaluation under BF/FL microscope
steps.instruction: Nuclei evaluation under BF/FL microscope: Mix 10 µL, nuclei suspension with 0.2 µL, 100X DAPI dye and 10 µL, 0.4% Trypan Blue dye . Inspect nuclei suspension under the bright-field and fluorescence microscope. Record the images ( Figure 1 ). Approximately 10 6 - 10 7 nuclei per 1 ml should be expected. When evaluating sample under the bright field there should be a lot of dark debris visible (Figure 1) making it hard to count the species. However, i the blue (DAPI) channel the nuclei should be clearly visible. Figure 1. Bright field microscopy image of nuclei suspension before FACS (blue circles indicate individual nuclei).

steps.title: Prepare the unstained nuclei sample
steps.instruction: Prepare the unstained nuclei sample: Take 50 µL, nuclei suspension and transfer to another 5 mL Falcon tube. Add 250 µL, Nuclei Resuspension Buffer . Label the tube as “reference” which will be used as a unstained population reference for FACS. Keep on ice

steps.title: Prepare the DAPI-stained nuclei sample
steps.instruction: Prepare the DAPI-stained nuclei sample: Take remaining, 950 µL, nuclei suspension and stain with 10 µL, 100X DAPI dye by mixing the suspension with 1 ml pipette. Keep on ice

steps.title: Prepare the FACS collection tube
steps.instruction: Prepare the FACS collection tube: Add 0.2 mL, Nuclei Resuspension Buffer into 5 mL Falcon tube and swirl buffer all around to wet the inner walls.

steps.title: and record the scatter plot
steps.instruction: and record the scatter plot. Run DAPI-positive nuclei suspenion (from Step 14), indetify the fluorescent population and record the scatter plot. Using the collection tube preapred in Step 15, sort the DAPI-postiive population into the collection tube The example of sorting gates (P

steps.title: Concentrate the nuclei Concentrate nuclei by...
steps.instruction: Concentrate the nuclei Concentrate nuclei by transferring FACS sorted suspension in 1.5 mL Protein LoBind tube and spinning in a swinging bucket centrifuge at 600 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes . Using FACS counts aim to reach a dilution of 2000 nuclei/ul.

steps.title: Count the nuclei After centrifugation carefully...
steps.instruction: Count the nuclei After centrifugation carefully aspirate the supernatant leaving the desirable amount of suspension (e.g. 200 µl) Carefully disperse the nuclei by mixing suspension with a 200 µl pipette. Mixi 10 µL, Nuclei Suspension with 0.2 µL, 100X DAPI dye and 10 µL, 0.4% Trypan Blue dye and count the nuclei on Countess II instrument. Expected results are shown in Figure 3 and Figure 4. Figure 3. Bright field microscopy image of lung nuclei suspension obtained after frozen tissue dissociation and FACS.

steps.title: Control experiment
steps.instruction: Control experiment: If frozen tissue dissociation is being conducted for the first time perform bulk cDNA synthesis and evaluate the cDNA yields and profile. Otherwise, proceed to the reverse transcription reaction using the platform and method of your choice.

steps.title: Barcoding, library preparation and sequencing Follow...
steps.instruction: Barcoding, library preparation and sequencing Follow 10X genomics protocol when using Chromium instrument 10X genomics protocol (v3.1 Chemistry) Follow inDrops protocol when using home-built inDrops plaform inDrops protocol

steps.title: and mapped to reference genome with...
steps.instruction: and mapped to reference genome with the default SEQC parameters to obtain the gene-cell count matrix. The expected results are : Frozen Human Lung - SCLC Figure

steps.title: snRNA-Seq of previously frozen human small cells lung carcinoma sample
steps.instruction: snRNA-Seq of previously frozen human small cells lung carcinoma sample. Frozen Human Lung - cSCLC Figure

steps.instruction: snRNA-Seq of previously frozen combined small cells lung carcinoma sample.
